meaning: `dIsIntE9mi:eIt/vi.<美>作非綿I`eIFEn/n.<美>酚间化投资,从银行蔷蛹浠(指由储蓄提取存款直接进行银行存款转为直接证券投资
examples: Then there is the looming threat of the Internet, with its tendency to disintermediate content from carrier.
But following those footsteps is a new wave of companies seeking not to disintermediate but rather to help traditional financial services adapt to today's changing landscape.
The internet retains the power to disintermediate (that is, bypass media firms by bringing products straight to consumers) and de-aggregate (turning albums into tracks and newspapers into articles).
Then there is the looming threat of the internet, with its tendency to disintermediate content from carrier.
Not only are Web-only stores out to disintermediate campus stores, but in some cases they're working directly with publishers.
